extracted_information: Docker uses Linux kernel mechanisms like namespaces, cgroups, and OverlayFS for container isolation. While these provide isolation, misconfigurations can create significant security vulnerabilities. Namespaces isolate what a process can 'see', cgroups control what a process can 'use' (resources), and OverlayFS manages the container's filesystem layers to ensure changes are container-specific and base layers remain untouched.

features.name: Namespaces
features.description: Isolates what a process can see or interact with in the system, creating an illusion of a dedicated environment.

types.name: pid
types.description: Isolates Process IDs (PIDs). Process thinks it's PID 1 (like init).

types.name: net
types.description: Isolates Network interfaces, IPs, routes. Gets its own 'private' network.

types.name: mnt
types.description: Isolates Mount points / filesystems. Sees only its own mounted directories.

types.name: uts
types.description: Isolates Hostname and domain name. Can set its own hostname.

types.name: ipc
types.description: Isolates Shared memory (semaphores, etc.). Prevents inter-process communication via shared memory.

types.name: user
types.description: Isolates UID/GID mappings. Can map its own root user differently.

types.name: cgroup
types.description: Isolates cgroup membership. Hides host resource limits.

features.name: Control Groups (cgroups)
features.description: Limits, measures, and isolates resource usage of processes or groups of processes, acting as 'resource police'.
capabilities: Limit CPU usage (e.g., to 1 core or 50% CPU time)
Cap RAM usage (and trigger OOM when exceeded)
Limit how many processes a container can spawn

features.name: OverlayFS (Union File System)
features.description: Simulates a complete filesystem by stacking multiple layers, preventing containers from trashing the disk or each other's data.
features.mechanism: Copy-on-write (changes are written only to the top writable layer)
benefits: Instant destruction and rebuilding (only writable layer is ephemeral)

features.name: Container Isolation Mechanisms (Summary)
features.description: Docker provides isolation by running processes with clever tricks built into the Linux kernel:
mechanisms: Control groups (what a process can use)
Overlay (Union) File Systems (what a process can write/interact with on disk)
